element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic used throughout all stages
Found as drops from candelabras; essential for boss encounters
Allows optional path selection affecting difficulty and rescue outcomes
Progressive difficulty; Death is notable mid-game spike
Optional objectives affecting ending; creates replay incentive
Ultimate challenge; final boss with multiple phases

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Classic Castlevania rhythm: gradual enemy/hazard buildup toward stage-end boss; boss serves as primary tension peak; password system allows strategic pause-points

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Branching paths offer alternative routes through stages; some paths lead to captive rescues, others to main progression

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible introduction to whip mechanics and basic platforming; teaches sub-weapon system
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased enemy density and hazard complexity; Death boss encounter represents notable spike in difficulty
difficulty_curve.late_game: Demanding platforming sections; final Dracula encounter with multiple attack phases; high punishment for mistakes
notable_spikes: Final stage approach to Dracula's chamber

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage terminates with mandatory boss fight; bosses have distinct attack patterns requiring sub-weapon strategy and whip timing
Multiple routes through stages affecting difficulty, pacing, and rescue opportunities; creates non-linear progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Not specified in sources; inferred from gameplay description

notable_level_patterns.description: Destructible candelabras yield sub-weapons and power-ups; encourages exploration and resource management

sources_used: Wikipedia - Castlevania: Dracula X (Gameplay, Plot, Reception sections)
The Cutting Room Floor - Castlevania: Dracula X (structural/technical info)
